title: Anchorage senator proposes expanding the Alaska parole board size, representation and transparency
summary: Corinne Smith Alaska Beacon An Anchorage senator has proposed expanding the Alaska Board of Parole, and widening the range of experiences of its members, citing the board’s workload and importance of fairness and accountability in its work. The parole board has granted far fewer people parole from state prisons in recent years. Since 2021, the board granted parole on average 25 percent of the time, with the other 75 percent denied or continued, according to a legislative audit last year. According to the ACLU of Alaska , the board has released 79 percent fewer people and held 75 percent fewer hearings than before the COVID-19 pandemic. Advocates argue the high rate of denials is contributing to overcrowding and a sense of hopelessness, also contributing to higher rates of suicide. There have been at least 67 deaths in Alaska since 2020 , with 17 reported as suicides. title: AST Residency Investigation: Anchor Point [AK24117041]
summary: Wildlife troopers in Anchor Point were contacted by conservation officers in New Mexico regarding possible residency violations occurring in Alaska. New Mexico conservation officers reported that a guide / outfitter from New Mexico may be obtaining Alaska resident hunting privileges illegally. Investigation determined that Franklin Keaton Waters 38 yoa of Artesia, New Mexico had obtained Alaska hunting / sportfishing licenses in 2023 and 2024 when he did not meet the definition of an Alaska resident. Waters was charged in Homer Court with Mutiple offenses including false Statement on an application, Failure to purchase locking tag, non-resident hunting without a guide, unlawful possession / transportation of game, failure to affix locking tag, seasons and bag limits for moose.

title: AST Vehicle Theft 1 / Fail to Stop / Parole Violation / Assault 3 / Reckless Endangerment / DUI: Fairbanks [AK25034212]
incident_type: Vehicle Theft 1 / Fail to Stop / Parole Violation / Assault 3 / Reckless Endangerment / DUI
summary: On April 12, 2025, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a stolen vehicle. The caller reported that he had picked up an unknown woman in Fairbanks near the Safeway off Old Steese Highway. The woman, later identified as 28-year-old Melissa Demit, remained in the Ford F-150 when the caller stopped at a gas station off Geist Road. When he returned outside, he reported that his truck was gone. A Trooper spotted the vehicle nearby and initiated a traffic stop as it entered the Mitchell Expressway. Demit refused to stop and led Troopers on a high-speed pursuit, frequently driving toward oncoming traffic on the Parks Highway.
